prompt: The lambda does not match the functional interface signature. What is the bug?
choices: Function cannot accept Integer type parameters
Function<Integer,Integer> expects a lambda with one parameter; (a,b)->a+b has two parameters and doesn't match the apply(T) signature
Lambda parameters must be declared with their types explicitly
Function should be BinaryOperator to support two-parameter lambdas
explanation: Function<T,R> represents T -> R (one input, one output). Its abstract method is R apply(T t). The lambda (a,b)->a+b has two parameters, which matches BiFunction<Integer,Integer,Integer>, not Function. Fix: use BiFunction<Integer,Integer,Integer> f = (a,b) -> a+b;
